behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is dispatcher->reviewer adapter handoff across modules. Concrete Unit miss: a role adapter can accidentally transform/truncate the document or map the wrong instruction-pack key even if the local payload-builder unit test passes. No real OS/network/subprocess is required (in-process fakes can capture requests), and correctness does not depend on model output content. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about "sends ... document" and "instruction pack," not live reviewer judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run dispatcher with three in-process fake reviewer clients that record invocations; assert all three received byte-identical source document content and each received its role-specific instruction pack identifier/content.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model extraction quality and non-deterministic output behavior. Concrete Integration miss: a stub extractor always returns expected terms, hiding real failures where the live model misses penalties or mislabels obligations. Rejected Workflow: behavior specifies one reviewer outcome, not multiple agentic stages chained together.
behaviors.test_description: Call the live clause-extractor model on a labeled contract set and assert required term categories are found with acceptance thresholds (e.g., per-category recall/minimum hit requirements) and correct evidence spans.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is wiring/serialization correctness across orchestrator and risk-assessor client modules. Concrete Unit miss: nested policy fields (exceptions, severity thresholds) can be dropped/renamed at module boundaries while individual helper functions still pass. No real OS/network is required; stubs can validate outgoing payload shape. Rejected Agentic/System: behavior text is about configuration passing, not model judgment or infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute orchestration path with a recording fake risk-assessor transport; assert outgoing invocation contains the full policy object (including nested fields), with correct names, types, and values.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Behavior evaluates semantic detection quality of a live model, especially indirect/euphemistic language that deterministic fixtures cannot represent reliably. Concrete Integration miss: mocked/stubbed assessor returns canned flags and will not reveal real false negatives on euphemisms. Rejected Workflow: this is a single reviewer decision quality check, not a multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live risk-assessor calls on a benchmark set containing explicit and euphemistic risky clauses; assert policy-aligned flagging/severity meets acceptance criteria and captures indirect risk expressions.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff (orchestrator -> compliance client payload). Concrete Unit miss: mapping logic can swap jurisdiction codes or omit profile fields during request assembly between modules. Real OS/network/model output is not required to catch this behavior. Rejected Agentic/System: behavior text is strictly about passing inputs correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Run compliance invocation through real orchestration code with a recording fake transport; verify jurisdiction and regulatory-profile fields are present, correctly mapped, and unmodified in the outbound request.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on live AI/ML interpretation of regulatory text against contract language. Concrete Integration miss: deterministic stubs always produce expected pass/fail and hide real misclassification or missed requirement evidence. Rejected Workflow: behavior targets one model's determination, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke live compliance checker on contracts with known regulatory pass/fail ground truth under configured profiles; assert determination accuracy and requirement-level citation coverage meet thresholds.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is merge logic for conflict resolution and attribution, which is local deterministic transformation. A focused unit test on merge function can catch overwrite/dedup bugs directly without I/O. Higher levels are optional confidence, not minimum. Rejected Integration: behavior text centers on "merge step preserves both assessments with attribution," which can be validated with direct structured inputs to the merge routine.
behaviors.test_description: Feed merge function synthetic reviewer outputs containing the same clause ID with conflicting labels; assert merged report keeps both assessments, each tagged with originating reviewer, and no conflict path overwrites the other.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a multi-step agent pipeline: parallel calls to three live AI reviewers plus downstream collection/merge. Concrete Integration miss: stubbing reviewers hides real output variability and timing/format differences that can cause dropped findings at handoff. Concrete Agentic miss (single-step): testing one reviewer live cannot expose cross-reviewer aggregation loss or conflict-resolution defects that appear only when multiple model outputs are combined.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline on real contracts with live clause/risk/compliance reviewers in parallel; assert final unified report contains findings from each reviewer, preserves conflicts with attribution, and shows no dropped reviewer findings between collection and merge.
